content.full_text:
Re
Farn PollrrcAl- PRACTTcES C oMMrssroN
P.O. Box 807 o 428 J Street o Sacramento, CA 95812-0807
(916) 322-5660 r Fax (916) 322-0886
May 30,2002
Kristin Parisi, Treasurer
Citizens for the
Preservation of Glendora
508 E Ada Avenue
Glendora, CA 91741
Your Request for Advice
Our File No. 4-02-112
Dear Ms. Parisi
QUESTION
Does your committee qualify as a sponsored committee, controlled committee, or
a general purpose committee?
CONCLUSION
It appears that your committee is a general purpose committee and also may be a
slate mailer or ganization.
' 
Government Code sections 81000 - 91014. Commission regulations appear at tifle 2, sections
18109 - 18997, of the California Code of Regulations.
This is in response to your letter requesting advice on behalf of Citizens for the
Preservation of Glendora regarding the type of committee your organizatron is considered
to be under the Political Reform Act (the "Act").l


Kristin Parisi
A-02-1t2
Page Two
FACTS
Cttizens for the Preservation of Glendora ("committee") was formed as a general
purpose committee in 1998 for the purpose of educating and informing the public on
local issues. The committee publishes a newspaper entitled Glendora Life that includes
local news, human interest stories, community events calendar, opinion columns, letters
to the editor, business advertisements, and political advertisements from candidates the
committee endorses. In addition, the newspaper solicits contributions to the committee
and takes editorial positions for and against candidates and issues. On occasion, the
publication has also included fliers for local businesses and political candidates. While
the newspaper was originally delivered by hand, since 2001, it is mailed to recipients.
In the last two local elections, the committee has received alarger portion of its
funding from candidates and candidate-controlled committees than in the past. The
larger amount is directly related to increased expenses, specifically, postal costs. The
candidates the committee endorsed, and another general purpose committee which also
endorsed the same candidates, were charged for ads, flier insertions, and distribution
expenses at a rate which covered printing and postage expenses.
ANALYSIS
A recipient committee is defined, in relevant part, by section 82013(a) as:
"... arry person or combination of persons who directly or indirectly
does any of the following:
(a) Receives contributions totaling one
thousand dollars ($1,000) or more in a calendar year."
A sponsored committee is defined by section 82048.7 as
"(a). . .a committee, other than a candidate controlled committee, which
has one or more sponsors. Any person, except a candidate or other
individual, may sponsor a committee.
(b) A person sponsors a committee if any of the following apply:
(1) 
The committee receives 80 percent or more
of its contributions from the person or its members,
offi cers, employees, or shareholders.
(2) 
The person collects contributions for the committee
by use of payroll deductions or dues from its
members, officers, or employees.


Kristin Parisi
A-02-rt2
Page Three
(3) 
The person alone or in combination with other
organizatrons, provides all or nearly all ofthe
administrative services for the committee.
(4) 
The person, alone or in combination with other
organizations, sets the policies for soliciting
contributions or making expenditures of committee
funds."
Nothing in your correspondence would lead us to believe that you qualify as a
sponsored committee. It appears that there is no separate organization that acts as a
sponsor to your committee. If there is additional information that was not included in
your letteq this issue may need to be reevaluated.
Section 82016 defines a controlled committee, in relevant part, as
"... a committee that is controlled directly or indirectly by a candidate or
state measure proponent or that acts jointly with a candidate, controlled
committee, or state measure proponent in connection with the making of
expenditures. A candidate or state measure proponent controls a
committee if he or she, his or her agent, or any other committee he or she
controls has a significant influence on the actions or decisions of the
committee."
We have advised that a candidate exerts significant influence on the actions of a
committee if the candidate develops or implements campaign strategy for the committee.
(lv[adden Advice Letter, No. A-85-197.) You specifically state in your letter, dated May
7,2002, that.
"With respect to the involvement of other committees in the format,
content, schedule, use of funds or method of distribution of our
Committee's publication, we remain independent. Our decisions have
never been made at the behest o{, or influenced by, any candidate or
committee."
Therefore, it appears that your committee would not meet the definition of a controlled
commrttee
Finally, a general purpose committee is defined in section 82027.5, in relevant
part, to mean all recipient committees which are "formed or exist[s] primarily to
support or oppose more than one candidate or ballot measure...." Your


Kristin Parisi
A-02-r12
Page Four
committee has established a history of supporting or opposing a variety of candidates
over time. Therefore, your committee meets the definition of a general purpose
committee.
It appears that the nature of your activity may have changed since our letter to you
on November 24, 1998 (Parisi Advice Letter No. 4-98-269). When you then wrote to
us, your publication was hand delivered. In our response to that letter, we defined a slate
mailer organization for you, but concluded that "the slate mailer provisions are not
applicable to your facts, provided that no more than200 newspapers are mailed." Since
you now mail your newspapers, we would like to revisit this issue.
A slate mailer refers to more than200 substantially similar pieces of mail that
support or oppose four or more candidates or ballot measures. (Section 82041.5,
82048.3 ) A "slate mailer organizatron" is an organization that receives $500 or more per
calendar year to produce slate mailers. (Section 82048.4.) While you did not specify
how much money was raised from candidates and others for the purpose of producing
and sending the newspapers, it appears that your committee may also qualify as a slate
mailer organization.
The Act imposes additional identification and reporting requirements on slate
mailer organizations. (Sections 84218, 84219,84305 5 and 84305.6.)
If you have additional questions, please feel free to contact me toll-free at
8661275-3772
Sincerely,
Luisa Menchaca
General
By
S
PhD
Political Reform Consultant II
Technical Assistance Division
